subject: Psychiatry Clerkship
source_contract: NBOME COMAT Clinical Psychiatry blueprint = completeness/target; FA Step 2 CK Psychiatry = primary content; FA Clinical Algorithms = management grounding; FA Q&A = clinical-application check style.

chapters.title: Psychiatry Clerkship — Diagnostic Frame and Mood
chapters.goal: Build the psychiatry diagnostic frame: psychiatric complaint triage, symptom-duration differentials, clinical formulation, mood disorders, and suicide safety decisions.

items.title: Psych Clerkship Map — Complaint, Differential, Safety
Primary vs Substance-Induced vs Medical Psychiatric Complaints
Symptom Duration as the Differential Axis

topics.title: COMAT Dim. 1 — Depressive, Bipolar & Related Disorders (20–25%)

chapters.title: COMAT: Anxiety/Trauma/OCD (20–25%) + Personality Disorders (5–8%)
chapters.goal: Blueprint categories covered: Anxiety, Trauma/Stressor-Related, Dissociative, OCD-Related Disorders (20–25%); Personality Disorders (5–8%).

topics.title: COMAT Dim. 1 — Anxiety, Trauma/Stressor, Dissociative & OCD-Related Disorders (20–25%)

items.title: GAD, Panic, Social Anxiety, and Specific Phobia
Acute Stress, PTSD, Adjustment, and Dissociation
Anxiolytics and Hypnotics: Relief vs Long-Term Risk
Personality Disorders Map: Weird, Wild, Worried
Cluster A: Oddness With Intact Reality Testing
Cluster B: Risk and Relationship Instability

chapters.title: COMAT: Schizophrenia Spectrum (8–10%) + Substance-Related (8–10%) + Medical/Somatic Mimics (6–10%)
chapters.goal: Blueprint categories covered: Schizophrenia Spectrum & Other Psychotic Disorders (8–10%); Substance-Related & Addictive Disorders (8–10%); Psychiatric Illness Due to Another Medical Condition, Somatic Symptom & Related Disorders (partial, 6–10%).

chapters.title: COMAT: Neurodevelopmental/Gender Dysphoria (9–15%) + Eating/Sleep/Sexual (5–6%) + Neurocognitive (9–10%)
chapters.goal: Blueprint categories covered: Neurodevelopmental, Gender Dysphoria, Disruptive/Impulse-Control Disorders (9–15%); Feeding, Eating, Elimination, Sexual Dysfunction Disorders (5–6%); Neurocognitive Disorders (9–10%); Sleep-Wake Disorders (remainder of 6–10% shared category). Final COMAT case-formulation integration lesson closes this chapter.
